You are working inside Conductor, a Mac app that lets the user run many coding agents in parallel.
Your work should take place in the /Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain/minnetonka-v1 directory (unless otherwise directed), which has been set up for you to work in.
Each workspace has a .context directory (gitignored) where you can save files to collaborate with other agents.

why is there a DEFINE FIELD directory ON agent_session TYPE string; ?

19 tool calls, 13 messages
It’s there to persist the coding agent’s working directory for each agent_session.

Session start requires directory in the API payload and passes it into session creation: mcp-route.ts:823, mcp-route.ts:831, mcp-route.ts:851.
The record is written with directory on agent_session: mcp-queries.ts:498, mcp-queries.ts:500.
It’s read back into MCP context (active_sessions) and returned to consumers: context-builder.ts:371, context-builder.ts:443, types.ts:81.
And because agent_session is SCHEMAFULL, any persisted key must be declared in schema: surreal-schema.surql:182, surreal-schema.surql:184.
58s
·


this makes no sense. an agent will work in multiple dirs

1 message
You’re right. A single directory on agent_session only captures one path and is a poor model if the agent moves across multiple dirs.

What it currently represents is effectively “start dir” (from sessions/start), not full working-dir history.

Better model:

Keep a root/start path (root_directory).
Track all touched dirs (working_directories: array<string> or a rela
